_comment: Tier-2 Layer-D: cross-backend numerical-consistency suite. Closes task #34. The Layer-C anchors (commits c84122e, 7973e80, 95c7f2b, 09e7239, etc.) verify each backend's catalog API converges to its own L2 error against the manufactured solution. Layer-D goes one step further: the SAME MMS produces NUMERICALLY CONSISTENT solutions across DIFFERENT backends. Single fixture runs both skfem (high-level forms + condense + solve) AND a manual scipy-P1 assembly (matches the kratos catalog scipy pattern) for poisson MMS u=sin(πx)·sin(πy) on [0,1]². Asserts pointwise max diff <= 5e-3 between skfem vs manual-scipy at 9 interior eval points AND each backend vs the analytic exact. Catches the class of bug that breaks one backend's catalog without breaking another (sign-convention errors, wrong material laws, off-by-one quadrature) — these would silently slip through individual Layer-C gates. Verified on 2026-06-01: skfem<>scipy max diff = 5.36e-4, both within 1.3e-3 of analytic exact. Filed under kratos / auxiliary_overview / pitfall_index=101 for runner-routing; the fixture is backend-agnostic but routes through repo .venv via the kratos slot to access the full MCP stack.
